rising.title: AI Agent Infrastructure: The Emerging Memory and State Management Layer
rising.latest_event: Baidu's 'Unlimited OCR' system (idx 11), which processes dozens of document pages in a single pass by modeling memory like human forgetting, represents a new architectural approach to agent memory — treating forgetting as a feature rather than a limitation — extending the agent memory infrastructure story into document processing and OCR.

rising.title: AI in Scientific Review and Research Automation
rising.latest_event: Synthetic Sciences has released OpenScience (idx 22), an open-source, model-agnostic AI workbench for ML, biology, physics, and chemistry research, entering as a direct open-source competitor to Claude Science Beta — which was described last week as a multi-agent workbench for reproducible genomics, proteomics, and cheminformatics pipelines.

rising.title: Google DeepMind
rising.latest_event: Google DeepMind and A24 have now formalized their relationship into an announced 'first-of-its-kind research partnership' (idx 25), a significant shift from last week's story of A24 managing user backlash over the collaboration — what was a reputational problem has been reframed as a deliberate institutional alliance; separately, Google DeepMind unionization talks are off to a rocky start (idx 12), introducing a new internal labor dimension to the DeepMind story.

rising.title: Open Source Models
rising.latest_event: llama.cpp has continued its near-daily build cadence, adding builds b9890 through b9894 (idx 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) this week, pushing further into the b9890s with no deceleration. Separately, Tencent has released Hy3, an open 295B Mixture-of-Experts model with 21B active parameters and 256K context (idx 77), adding a major new open-weight capability release to the ecosystem.

rising.title: Autoresearch and Autonomous Self-Improvement: The Agent-Trains-Agent Frontier
rising.latest_event: NVIDIA HORIZON, a hands-free agent that autonomously evolves Git worktrees and achieves 100% RTL benchmark completion (idx 18), is the most concrete autoresearch/autonomous self-improvement artifact yet — a named, shipped system from a major hardware company that closes the loop between agent autonomy and engineering output.

rising.title: Anthropic's Hardware Independence Strategy: Custom Silicon and Samsung
rising.latest_event: The Anthropic-Samsung chip discussions are confirmed again in the Meta Watermelon digest (idx 11), maintaining narrative continuity but without new details — the story remains in the reported-but-unconfirmed stage.
